description: Native Swift iOS App — Phase 8: Smart Suggestions, Offline Cache, Deep Links & Capacitor Retirement. Port the React suggestions engine (18 pairings + frequency + recency), add basic offline read caching with stale data indicators, handle Universal Link auth callbacks, and retire the Capacitor WebView shell.

userStories.title: SuggestionEngine — Port React Algorithm to Swift
userStories.description: Port the smart item suggestion algorithm from React to Swift, including pairing-based, frequency-based, and recency-based strategies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SuggestionEngine with static ITEM_PAIRINGS: [(String, String)] — all 18 pairs from React, exact strings
getSuggestions(history:currentItems:maxSuggestions:) -> [ItemSuggestion]
ItemSuggestion struct: name: String, reason: String, category: String
Algorithm: Build currentNames Set<String> (lowercased) from current items
Pairings: For each pair, if one side is in currentNames and other is not, suggest the other with reason 'Often bought together'
Frequency: Count all history entries by lowercased name. Top 20 by count. Each with count ≥ 2 not in currentNames or already suggested → reason 'Purchased N times before'
Recency: Last 30 history entries, reverse, deduplicate (first occurrence). Each not in currentNames or already suggested → reason 'Recently purchased'
Deduplicate across all three strategies (first-wins by lowercased name)
Auto-categorize each via CategoryDefinitions.categorizeItem()
Return up to maxSuggestions (default 8)
Case-insensitive matching throughout
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: SuggestionEngine enum with 18 pairings, 3-phase algorithm (pairing/frequency/recency), ItemSuggestion model. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Core algorithm with 3 strategies, deduplication, and case-insensitive matching — must match React exactly

userStories.title: SuggestionsView — Chip Grid UI
userStories.description: Display suggestion chips with category-colored dots, collapsible at 4 items.
acceptanceCriteria: SuggestionsView component with header: 'AI Suggestions' bold title + 'Based on your shopping habits' secondary subtitle
Grid of chips, each showing: category-colored circle (8pt) + item name + reason text (smaller, secondary) + '+' icon
Tapping a chip calls onAdd(name:) callback
Collapsible: initially shows 4 chips; if more exist, 'Show N more' button expands; toggles to 'Show less'
If no suggestions, render nothing (hidden)
Chip styling: rounded rect, surface background, subtle border, category dot color from CategoryDefinitions
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: SuggestionsView with LazyVGrid chip layout, category-colored dots, expand/collapse at 4 items, ultraThinMaterial styling. Build succeeds.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Custom chip grid UI with expand/collapse and category color integration

userStories.title: ListDetailView — Wire Suggestions
userStories.description: Integrate suggestions into the shopping list view, displayed above items.
acceptanceCriteria: In ListDetailView, compute suggestions via SuggestionEngine.getSuggestions() passing history and unchecked items
Recompute when items or history change
Display SuggestionsView above the item list (below add-item bar, above first store/category group)
onAdd handler: call viewModel.addItem(name:) — same as manual add
If the list has 0 history entries, suggestions section is hidden entirely
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Wired SuggestionEngine + SuggestionsView into ListDetailView. AI suggestions section appears as first section in item list, hidden when no history. onAdd uses addItem with selectedStoreId.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Wiring suggestions computation to list view with reactive updates

userStories.title: OfflineCache — Local Data Persistence
userStories.description: Create a generic caching layer using FileManager + JSON encoding for offline data access.
acceptanceCriteria: Create OfflineCache using FileManager + JSON encoding
save<T: Encodable>(key:data:) → encode to JSON, write to app's caches directory
load<T: Decodable>(key:) -> T? → read from caches directory, decode
Keys: lists-{userId}, items-{listId}, stores-{userId}, history-{userId}
Each cached entry includes cachedAt: Date timestamp
Cache writes happen automatically after every successful Supabase fetch (non-blocking, background)
Thread-safe access (actor or serial queue)
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: OfflineCache actor with CachedEntry<T> wrapper for timestamped JSON caching. FileManager-backed, thread-safe via Swift actor. save/load/remove/clearAll methods.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Generic cache with thread safety, JSON serialization, and timestamp tracking

userStories.title: ViewModels — Cache Integration
userStories.description: Integrate offline cache into ViewModels for instant cached data display with fresh data fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: ListViewModel: On init, load cached lists immediately, then fetch from Supabase. If fetch succeeds, update cache. If fetch fails (offline), keep cached data.
ListDetailViewModel: On init, load cached items for the active list, then fetch. Same fallback pattern.
StoreViewModel: Same pattern for stores.
When showing cached data and NetworkMonitor.isConnected is false, set isShowingCachedData: Bool flag
When connection returns and fresh data loads, clear the flag and update cache
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: Integrated OfflineCache into ListViewModel, ListDetailViewModel, and StoreViewModel. Cache-first pattern: load cached data instantly, then fetch live. On success → update cache. On failure → keep cached + set isShowingCachedData flag.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Cache-first loading pattern across 3 ViewModels with network-aware state flags

userStories.title: Stale Data Indicator
userStories.description: Show a subtle banner when viewing cached data so users know information might be outdated.
acceptanceCriteria: When isShowingCachedData is true on any view model: show a subtle banner below navigation title: 'Showing cached data · Last updated [relative time]'
Light yellow/amber background, small text
Relative time: 'just now', '5m ago', '1h ago', 'yesterday', etc. from cachedAt timestamp
Banner disappears immediately when fresh data loads
Banner appears below the offline banner (from Phase 7) if both are visible — offline banner takes priority position
Build succeeds
userStories.notes: CachedDataBanner component with amber styling, relative time formatting (just now/Xm ago/Xh ago/yesterday/Xd ago). Wired into ListBrowserView, ListDetailView, StoreBrowserView. cachedAt property added to all three ViewModels.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Simple conditional banner with relative time formatting

userStories.title: DeepLinkHandler — onOpenURL for Auth Callbacks
userStories.description: Handle Universal Links for auth callbacks so OAuth and email confirmation flows complete seamlessly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add .onOpenURL modifier to root WindowGroup in GatherListsApp.swift
When URL contains auth/callback or fragment #access_token: parse URL fragment to extract access_token and refresh_token
Call SupabaseManager.shared.client.auth.session(from:) or manually set session via setSession(accessToken:refreshToken:)
AuthViewModel will pick up the change via authStateChanges listener
When URL doesn't match auth patterns: ignore (log for debugging)
Handle both https://gatherlists.com/auth/callback#... and gather://auth/callback#... URL schemes
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Auth flow with URL parsing, token extraction, and session restoration — security-sensitive

userStories.title: AuthViewModel — setSession Support
userStories.description: Add deep link handling to AuthViewModel for session restoration from URL tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: Add handleDeepLink(url:) method to AuthViewModel
Parse the URL for auth callback indicators
Extract tokens from URL fragment
Call Supabase SDK's session restoration method
Existing authStateChanges listener handles the rest (updates isAuthenticated, currentUser, fetches profile)
Handle errors gracefully — if tokens are invalid, log and ignore (user stays on login screen)
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Auth session restoration from deep link tokens — must handle invalid/expired tokens gracefully

userStories.title: RecipeViewModel — Offline Cache
userStories.description: Add offline cache support to recipes and collections for viewing while cooking without internet.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeViewModel: Load cached collections + recipes on init, then fetch fresh
Cache keys: collections-{userId}, recipes-{collectionId}
Same isShowingCachedData pattern as US-005
Stale data indicator in CollectionBrowserView and CollectionRecipeListView
Build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Cache integration following established pattern from US-005

userStories.title: Kill Capacitor — Remove WebView Shell
userStories.description: Remove the Capacitor iOS app configuration and all related dependencies, shipping only the native Swift app.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove ios/ directory (the Capacitor Xcode project)
Remove Capacitor dependencies from package.json: @capacitor/app, @capacitor/core, @capacitor/ios, @capacitor/splash-screen, @capacitor/status-bar, @capacitor-community/apple-sign-in
Remove capacitor.config.ts
Remove AppUrlListener.jsx component and its usage in App.jsx
Remove Capacitor platform checks: any Capacitor.isNativePlatform() guards, Capacitor.getPlatform() checks
Update vite.config.js if it has Capacitor-specific config
Update usePWAInstall.js to remove Capacitor platform detection (PWA install banner should show on all web platforms)
Run npm install to update lockfile
Run npm run build to verify React app still builds
Keep: public/.well-known/apple-app-site-association (still needed for native Swift app's Universal Links)
Keep: The React PWA continues to work at gatherlists.com for web/desktop users
userStories.notes: This is a React codebase story, not Swift
userStories.testIntensityReason: Removing major dependency — must verify no runtime breakage in React PWA

userStories.title: Post-Capacitor Cleanup — Verify React PWA
userStories.description: Verify the React PWA works correctly after Capacitor removal for web/desktop users.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build succeeds with zero errors
No runtime imports of @capacitor/* packages remain in the bundle
PWA install banner still works on Android (via beforeinstallprompt) and iOS Safari (instructional)
Apple Sign-In still works via web OAuth redirect (Supabase handles the redirect, not Capacitor)
All Supabase auth flows (sign-in, sign-up, sign-out) work in the browser
Service worker registration still functions
No console errors related to missing Capacitor plugins
userStories.notes: This is a React codebase story, not Swift
userStories.testIntensityReason: Comprehensive verification after major dependency removal — auth, PWA, and build integrity
